info.name: Rule Engine API
info.description: Rule Engine REST API aligned with FastAPI routes: execution (optional consumer_id), workflows, management CRUD, DMN, versioning, A/B tests (trailing slash on list/create), hot reload HTTP API and WebSocket /ws/hot-reload, consumers at /consumers (no /api/v1 prefix). See /docs for schemas.

item.name: Health & Info

request.description: Check the health status of the API service.
Root endpoint that provides API information.
Hot reload subsystem health: monitoring, registry stats, and structured checks.

item.description: Health check and API information endpoints

item.name: Rules Execution

request.description: Execute business rules against input data and return scoring results with action recommendations.
Execute rules in dry run mode to see detailed rule evaluations without side effects.
Execute business rules from a specific ruleset against input data. Set the `ruleset_name` variable to the target ruleset (e.g. `default_ruleset`).
Execute a specific ruleset in dry run mode to preview detailed per-rule evaluations without side effects.
Execute rules against multiple input data items in parallel for efficient batch processing.

item.description: Rule execution endpoints for single and batch operations

item.name: DMN Rules Execution

request.description: Execute business rules from a DMN file (via file path) against input data.
Execute business rules from DMN XML content against input data.

formdata.description: Optional consumer ID for usage tracking

request.description: Execute business rules from an uploaded DMN file against input data.
Execute DMN rules in dry run mode to see detailed rule evaluations.

item.description: DMN (Decision Model Notation) rule execution endpoints

request.description: Execute a multi-stage workflow with the provided process name, stages, and input data.
Execute workflow with default stages (INITIATED, NEW, INPROGESS, FINISHED).
Run a stored, active workflow definition by name; stages are taken from the saved workflow.

item.name: Workflow Definitions

request.description: Create a workflow definition (201). Stages must be a non-empty ordered list.
List workflow definitions with optional filters and pagination.
Get a single workflow definition by name.
Update description, stages, and/or is_active.
Soft delete (deactivate) by default. Use query hard=true if supported for hard delete.

query.description: Optional hard delete when implementation supports it

request.description: DELETE with hard=true when the service supports hard deletion.

item.description: CRUD for stored workflow definitions under /api/v1/workflows

item.name: DMN Upload

request.description: Upload a DMN (Decision Model Notation) XML file and convert it to the rule engine's internal rule format.

item.description: DMN file upload and parsing endpoints

request.description: Retrieve a list of all configured rules.
Retrieve a specific rule by its identifier.
Create a new rule with the provided configuration.
Update an existing rule with new configuration.
Delete a rule by its identifier.
Retrieve a list of all configured conditions.
Retrieve a specific condition by its identifier.
Create a new condition with the provided configuration.
Update an existing condition with new configuration.
Delete a condition by its identifier.
Retrieve a list of all configured actions/patterns.
Retrieve a specific action by its pattern string.
Create a new action/pattern with the provided configuration.
Update an existing action/pattern with a new message.
Delete an action/pattern by its pattern string.
List attributes (facts) used in conditions and input data.
Get one attribute by attribute_id.
Create an attribute (201). data_type examples: string, number, integer, boolean, date, array, object.
Partial update of an attribute.
Delete an attribute by ID (204).
Retrieve a list of all configured rulesets.
Retrieve a specific ruleset by its name.
Create a new ruleset with the provided configuration. Note: RuleSets are logical groupings and validation only.
Update an existing ruleset with new configuration. Note: RuleSets are logical groupings and validation only.
Delete a ruleset by its name. Note: This is a logical operation that validates the deletion.

item.name: Rule Versioning

query.description: Maximum number of versions to return

request.description: Retrieve the complete version history for a specific rule.
Retrieve the current (latest) version of a specific rule.
Retrieve a specific version of a rule by version number.
Compare two versions of a rule to see differences.
Rollback a rule to a specific previous version.

item.description: Rule version history, compare, and rollback endpoints

item.name: Hot Reload

request.description: Current hot reload service status. Real-time updates also available via WebSocket: ws://HOST:PORT/ws/hot-reload
Reload rules from database into memory. Field validate aliases validate_before_reload.
Reload one rule by ID.
Reload all rules in a ruleset (numeric ruleset ID).
Validate rules currently held in memory.
Load from config repository (e.g. DB when USE_DATABASE=true) and validate. Optional source: ruleset name or file path.
Start automatic hot reload monitoring (interval 5–3600 seconds).
Stop hot reload monitoring thread.
Recent reload history entries.

item.description: Hot reload HTTP API under /api/v1/rules/hot-reload. WebSocket: /ws/hot-reload

item.name: Consumers

request.description: List consumers. Path is /consumers (no /api/v1 prefix).
Get one consumer by consumer_id string.
Create consumer (201).
Update consumer fields (partial).
Delete consumer (204).

item.description: Consumer registry at /consumers (matches FastAPI router prefix)

item.name: A/B Testing

body.raw:
{
  "test_id": "test-001",
  "test_name": "Rule variant comparison",
  "rule_id": "R0001",
  "ruleset_id": 1,
  "variant_a_version": "v1",
  "variant_b_version": "v2",
  "description": "Compare v1 vs v2 for conversion",
  "traffic_split_a": 0.5,
  "traffic_split_b": 0.5,
  "variant_a_description": "Control (current)",
  "variant_b_description": "Treatment (new)",
  "duration_hours": 168,
  "min_sample_size": 100,
  "confidence_level": 0.95
}

request.description: Create a new A/B test to compare two rule versions (POST /api/v1/rules/ab-tests/).

query.description: Maximum number of tests to return

request.description: List all A/B tests with optional filters.
Retrieve details of a specific A/B test.
Start a draft A/B test.
Stop a running A/B test and optionally declare a winner (A or B).
Retrieve metrics and analysis for an A/B test.
Assign an assignment key (e.g. user ID, session ID) to a variant using hash-based routing.
Delete a draft A/B test (only tests in draft status can be deleted).

item.description: A/B test creation, listing, start/stop, metrics, variant assignment, and deletion
